channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FCI Tightens Oversight on Subsidized Rice Use by Distilleries
item.description: The Food Corporation of India (FCI) has announced stricter measures to ensure the proper use of subsidized rice by distilleries involved in ethanol production. This comes amid concerns about misuse of...

item.title: Man arrested over death of former UK politician Ann Widdecombe
item.description: British police have arrested a 26-year-old white British man on suspicion of murdering Ann Widdecombe, the 78-year-old former government minister and Reform UK politician. Widdecombe was discovered de...

item.title: Kashmir's environment needs protection from tourism overload
item.description: The article discusses the growing concern over unregulated tourism in Kashmir and its impact on the region's natural beauty and cultural heritage. Officials, including Omar, emphasize the need for str...

item.title: Oyo abduction: Eight suspects arrested in coordinated rescue operation
item.description: The Special Adviser to the President on Information and Strategy, Bayo Onanuga, revealed that eight suspected kidnappers involved in the abduction of pupils and teachers in Orire Local Government Area...

item.title: Sigabo Episode 15: Gabo at Seb's intense clash over Sam's storyline
item.description: The article discusses the intense confrontation between characters Gabo and Seb in the TV series 'Sigabo,' Episode 15, which escalated due to tensions involving the character Sam. The episode highligh...

item.title: Fraser Canyon wildfires threaten residents and property
item.description: Wildfires in British Columbia's Fraser Canyon are escalating, posing significant risks to residents and properties. The Brunswick Complex fires have intensified, with the Ainslie Creek fire now spanni...

item.title: Natural Gas Gains Traction as Fuel Prices Remain High
item.description: The article discusses how increasing traditional fuel costs are leading to a shift towards natural gas as a vehicle fuel option. Transportation costs are rising due to persistent fuel price hikes, pro...

item.title: Survey Shows Majority of Filipinos Back National Government's Stance on West Philippine Sea Disputes
item.description: A recent survey by Octa Research reveals that 66% of adult Filipinos strongly support the national government's response to territorial disputes in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) with China. The survey...

item.title: Turkish President Gifts NATO Leaders with Pistols, Sparks Diplomatic Debate
item.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan made an unexpected diplomatic gesture during a NATO summit by presenting personalized handguns to alliance leaders. This act, rooted in Turkish cultural traditio...

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Upholds Illinois Ban on Semiautomatic Guns
item.description: A federal appeals court has ruled to uphold Illinois' controversial ban on semiautomatic firearms, rejecting a challenge that argued the law was unconstitutional. The 7th Circuit Court of Appeals reve...

item.title: Prince Harry reunites with King Charles and grandchildren after four-year gap
item.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex, along with their children Prince Archie and Princess Lilibet, have visited King Charles and Queen Camilla at Highgrove House, marking the first in-person meeting betwee...

item.title: NCERT blacklists firm for delayed textbook production; probe ordered
item.description: The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) faced legal scrutiny after failing to defend its decision to blacklist Pune-based Bafna Global Venture Private Limited for delaying te...

item.title: Weekly Quiz: Test Your Knowledge with Fun Trivia Questions
item.description: The article presents a weekly quiz featuring 20 diverse questions covering topics such as color theory, sports events, mental health professionals, historical figures like Nelson Mandela, geographical...

item.title: California senator criticizes Canada's wine import restrictions amid trade tensions
item.description: The article discusses concerns raised by a California senator about the impact of Canada's boycott of U.S. wines. The senator argues that the trade restrictions are causing significant harm to the Cal...

item.title: Tyler Robinson's murder trial delayed as judge postpones decision until September
item.description: The pre-trial hearing for Tyler Robinson, accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk, concluded without a verdict. Utah District Judge Tony Graf postponed his decision until September 1, citing the need fo...

item.title: SK Hynix's IPO Raises $26.5 Billion as Memory Chip Shortages Persist
item.description: SK Hynix, the South Korean memory chip giant, made history with its $26.5 billion IPO on Wall Street, marking the largest foreign company debut in the US. The offering, which priced at $149 per Americ...

item.title: Celebration Church International marks six months in permanent global HQ, membership surpasses 30,000
item.description: Celebration Church International (CCI) has achieved significant milestones six months after establishing its permanent global headquarters. The church, founded in Lagos in 2012 by Apostle Emmanuel Ire...

item.title: Spurs Remove Jacob Tobey as Announcer Following Affair Allegations
item.description: Jacob Tobey, the lead play-by-play announcer for the San Antonio Spurs, has been dismissed from his position following allegations of an affair that surfaced on social media. The controversy began whe...

item.title: Oyo State Government Commends Security Agencies for Rescuing Kidnapped Students and Teachers
item.description: Eight weeks after gunmen abducted dozens of pupils and teachers from three schools in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, the victims have been released safely. The state government praised the...

item.title: WA Avoids Comment on Taiwan's Proposed Perth Office Amid Diplomatic Tensions
item.description: Western Australia is refusing to comment on whether it supports Taiwan's push to establish a new office in Perth, as the Albanese government appears poised to approve the proposal despite growing dipl...
